title: campanula velutina var. gibraltarica
intro: Campanula velutina var. gibraltarica, also known as the Gibraltar bellflower, is a variety of flowering plant in the Campanula genus. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its roots and leaves are valued for their medicinal properties, including their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Campanula velutina var. gibraltarica include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to soothe sore throats and reduce fever. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its availability and usage may vary due to its regional distribution and traditional practices.
